patch.value: ADR: explicit pm_format_version front-matter field for storage schema evolution
Adopt an explicit, monotonically increasing item front-matter format version (pm_format_version) to gate future breaking storage migrations, replacing reliance on structural detection alone.

## Context

TOON item front-matter had no explicit format/schema version. When a breaking front-matter change ships, there is no per-item gate to distinguish items needing migration from already-migrated items; `migrateItemFilesToFormat` relied on structural detection (file extension / presence of a legacy `.md` alternate). Tracked by pm-ae1u under the storage epic pm-o2kc. Related shipped work: legacy markdown→TOON migration (pm-w5j7), first-mutation auto-migration (pm-yvwt), legacy format gate (pm-z8bl) — all about the *file format* (md vs toon) and *settings* version, NOT a within-TOON front-matter schema version.

## Decision

Add an explicit, monotonically increasing integer `pm_format_version` to item front-matter, owned by `core/item/item-format-version.ts` (`CURRENT_ITEM_FORMAT_VERSION`, `BASELINE_ITEM_FORMAT_VERSION`, `effectiveItemFormatVersion`, `classifyItemFormatVersion`, `scanItemFormatVersions`). Keep structural md→toon migration orthogonal.

Token economy is a first-class constraint: the baseline version (1) is the implicit default and is NEVER serialized (`normalizeItemFormatVersion` drops it). The field only materializes once an item advances past the baseline. Absence therefore always means baseline — now and after future bumps. The entire current corpus gains zero bytes and zero git churn.

Surfacing: `pm validate` gains a default `format_version` check (outdated => warn, ahead => error because validation cannot vouch for fields a newer pm wrote); `pm health` integrity check tallies `item_outdated_format_version` / `item_ahead_format_version`. Both resolve to remediation registry guidance (migration listing / pm upgrade).

## Rationale

- A version stamp makes future migration a per-item O(1) decision instead of a full-corpus structural re-parse.
- Omitting the baseline keeps the agent-facing context minimal, honoring the project's token-efficiency mandate, while still providing a forward/backward safety gate.
- The `ahead` guard protects against silent mishandling when an older pm reads items written by a newer pm (downgrade scenario).

## Consequences

- At the baseline version the outdated path is unreachable by construction (an effective version below 1 cannot occur); its per-item mapping in health is c8-ignored and covered by the pure unit tests of the helper. When `CURRENT_ITEM_FORMAT_VERSION` is bumped to 2, the outdated path activates and a migration that rewrites sub-current items must accompany the bump.
- Parse validation only enforces shape (integer >= 1), never range, so reading is never bricked by an ahead version; range is advisory via health/validate.

## Alternatives considered

- Keep structural detection only: rejected — cannot stage multi-version migrations and cannot detect newer-than-runtime files.
- Eagerly stamp every item at version 1: rejected — adds a line to all ~1100 items (token + churn cost) for zero present value.
